title: Pick the Right AI for the Job

modules.title: Same Question, Five Different Answers
modules.sub: Discover how different AI models respond to identical prompts
modules.description: You step into the role of an AI consultant and run the same prompt through multiple AI models on behalf of five very different clients. By comparing the outputs side by side, you begin to notice patterns in tone, accuracy, and usefulness that vary significantly between tools. This sets up the central question of the course: why does the right AI depend so heavily on who is asking and why?

modules.title: What Are Benchmarks Actually Measuring?
modules.sub: Look behind the leaderboard scores to find real meaning
modules.description: You examine the benchmark scores and performance claims that AI companies use to rank their models, then interrogate what those numbers actually capture — and what they leave out. Through guided analysis, you identify the gap between lab performance and real-world usefulness for specific tasks. This module equips you with a critical lens so you can read AI marketing claims without being misled.

modules.title: Build Your Own Testing Rubric
modules.sub: Design a fair, repeatable way to evaluate any AI model
modules.description: You create a structured rubric tailored to one of your five clients, choosing criteria that genuinely matter for their specific task — such as accuracy, creativity, safety, or speed. You then run live tests using your rubric and record evidence-based scores for each AI model you evaluate. By the end, you have a reusable evaluation tool that works beyond this course.

modules.title: The Tool Recommendation Report
modules.sub: Make your final call and defend it with real evidence
modules.description: You compile your findings into a professional-style recommendation report for all five clients, justifying each AI selection using the rubric data you gathered. The story reaches its conclusion as your choices play out with consequences inside the scenario, making the stakes of a well-reasoned recommendation feel real. You leave with both a completed deliverable and a decision framework you can apply to any future AI selection challenge.

synopsis: You are a consultant hired to choose the best AI model for five very different clients — a novelist, a coder, a journalist, a student, and a small business owner. Every choice you make has real consequences inside the story, and by the end you will have built a reusable decision framework you can apply outside it.
rationale: Teenagers are already choosing between AI tools without any framework for evaluation, making them vulnerable to hype, marketing, and benchmark manipulation rather than genuine task-fit thinking.
learning_outcome: Students can evaluate at least three AI models against a self-designed rubric for a specific task and justify their recommendation with evidence from structured testing.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
Top 5 Copilot AI features for students - Microsoft
